channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Urgent repairs and safety measures needed at deadly bridge blackspot
item.description: This editorial focuses on a notorious bridge in Kenya that has gained a grim reputation as a ‘killer’ crossing due to frequent accidents and repeated loss of life. It argues that Kenyans should not ha...

item.title: Severe Windstorm Topples Semi-Trucks and Closes Part of Coquihalla Highway in B.C.
item.description: A powerful windstorm swept through British Columbia’s Fraser Valley and Hope area on Wednesday night, producing winds equivalent to a Category 1 hurricane. The extreme gusts, which peaked at 139 km/h ...

item.title: CBK Reports Stable Kenyan Shilling Backed by KSh 1.9 Trillion Forex Reserves
item.description: According to the Central Bank of Kenya’s latest data, the Kenyan shilling has maintained stability against the US dollar and other East African Community (EAC) currencies. As of Friday, March 13, 2026...

item.title: Ombudsman to file charges against eight House members over flood control fund irregularities
item.description: The Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) has recommended filing criminal charges against eight members of the House of Representatives regarding anomalies in flood control projects, Ombudsm...

item.title: March Break Activities and Events in Ottawa
item.description: The article provides a comprehensive overview of activities and events available in Ottawa over March Break, offering options for families, students, and tourists. It highlights local parks reopening ...

item.title: Tanzania and Uganda Start Talks to Strengthen Cooperation in Politics, Trade, and Agriculture
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania na Uganda wameshitisha kuanza mazungumzo ya pamoja kwa lengo la kuimarisha ushirikiano katika maeneo muhimu ya maendeleo ya kikanda. Mazungumzo haya, yaliyoanza rasmi tarehe 1...

item.title: Bombay High Court Reviews Petition on Domestic LPG Supply Amid Export Concerns
item.description: The Nagpur bench of the Bombay High Court on Thursday directed the Union government and Confidence Petroleum India Limited to respond to a petition filed by six LPG distributors. The distributors have...

item.title: Courier Mail digitally alters image of Tony Burke in news story on Iranian footballers
item.description: In a striking example of media editing, the Courier Mail in Brisbane digitally removed Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke from a photograph featuring Iranian women’s football players, despite the same i...

item.title: Nadra Confirms Digital Identity Cards Are Legally Valid Like Physical CNICs
item.description: The National Database and Registration Authority (Nadra) has clarified that digital identity cards are legally equivalent to physical CNICs. Citizens using digital identity cards should not be asked t...

item.title: Toronto Food Bank Shipment Delayed After Incident in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A shipment of rice intended for Toronto’s Daily Bread Food Bank was affected by a strike in the Strait of Hormuz near Iran, according to CEO Neil Hetherington. The shipment included six containers, ea...

item.title: Guide to Booking LPG Cylinders via WhatsApp, Mobile Apps and Other Methods
item.description: In India, LPG distributors have made it simpler for consumers to book gas cylinders without waiting in long queues. With supply concerns causing occasional delays, companies like Indane, HP Gas, and B...

item.title: Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan Discuss Filming the Peaky Blinders Movie
item.description: The Peaky Blinders film, 'The Immortal Man', picks up six years after the series finale, set in 1940 Birmingham under the threat of Nazi bombing. Cillian Murphy reprises his role as Tommy Shelby, now ...

item.title: Nigerian Army Arrests Cattle Rustlers, Neutralises Terrorists and Rescues Kidnapped Bank Staff in Benue

item.title: Technical Committee to Decide Policy on Day Senior Schools Amid Uncertainty
item.description: The future of day senior schools in Kenya hangs in balance as the Ministry of Education hands over key policy decisions to a technical committee. This comes amid ongoing transition to Competency-Based...

item.title: Halifax Council Orders Demolition of Two Illegal Storeys from Dartmouth Building
item.description: Halifax Regional Council has ordered a developer in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia, to dismantle the top two storeys of a residential building that were built without official approval. The building at 169 Wy...

item.title: SADC Calls for Stronger Regional Cooperation to Shield Economies from Global Crises
item.description: Minister of International Relations and Cooperation Ronald Lamola has emphasised the urgent need for Southern African countries to deepen regional cooperation in order to safeguard their economies aga...

item.title: Budget impact of negative gearing and capital gains tax concessions projected to double by 2035
item.description: New analysis from the Parliamentary Budget Office (PBO) shows that the cost to the Australian Budget from negative gearing and capital gains tax concessions for property investors is set to almost dou...

item.title: US Conducts Rescue Operation Following Military Aircraft Accident in Iraq
item.description: On Thursday, a US military refuelling aircraft, Boeing KC‑135 Stratotanker, crashed in western Iraq during an operational mission under Operation Epic Fury. The US Central Command confirmed that the i...

item.title: Opposition Moves to Remove Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, Submits Notices in Parliament
item.description: In a significant political development, the opposition has formally submitted notices seeking the removal of Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar to the presiding officers of both Houses of...

item.title: Gurpreet Singh moved to special jail unit amid links to Toronto police corruption investigation
item.description: Gurpreet Singh, alleged drug smuggler associated with accused trafficking kingpin Ryan Wedding, has been transferred to a special handling unit at the Toronto South Detention Centre. The move occurred...
